journal_entry.description: Major overhaul of machinery extending useful life
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS (IAS 16), expenditure that extends the useful life of an asset beyond its original estimate is capitalized as it enhances the asset's future economic benefits. The $200,000 overhaul cost is added to the carrying amount of the machinery (asset increase) rather than expensed. Cash is reduced by the payment amount. No tax lines are included as capital expenditures on owned assets are not subject to sales tax recovery in the same transaction (any tax paid would have been included in the stated $200,000 amount).

output.reason: The $200,000 cash payment for the major overhaul represents an asset decrease (cash leaving the business). The overhaul is capitalized (added to the machinery asset account on the debit side) because it extends useful life, but on the credit side we only record the cash outflow. One credit line: asset decrease.

credit_classifier.reason: The $200,000 cash payment for the major overhaul represents an asset decrease (cash leaving the business). The overhaul is capitalized (added to the machinery asset account on the debit side) because it extends useful life, but on the credit side we only record the cash outflow. One credit line: asset decrease.

output.reason: The $200,000 major overhaul extends the useful life of the machinery, which under IFRS qualifies as a capital improvement that should be capitalized (added to the asset). This is 1 asset increase (debit to Machinery or similar fixed asset account). The original purchase is not part of this transaction - only the year 5 overhaul expenditure is being recorded here.

debit_classifier.reason: The $200,000 major overhaul extends the useful life of the machinery, which under IFRS qualifies as a capital improvement that should be capitalized (added to the asset). This is 1 asset increase (debit to Machinery or similar fixed asset account). The original purchase is not part of this transaction - only the year 5 overhaul expenditure is being recorded here.
